name: Riley
manager_notes: Riley is in week two and is moving quickly through codebase orientation, but he has mostly learned from docs and async notes rather than direct team contact. I want to check whether he feels clear on ownership boundaries, incident expectations, and where to ask for context before making architecture assumptions. There is a risk he looks productive while building an internal map that differs from how the team actually operates. This check-in should identify missing context early and convert it into concrete onboarding support.
answers: I am comfortable reading the services, but I am less sure how decisions are really made here.
I can trace most request paths now, though I still miss historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
I am trying not to interrupt people, so sometimes I keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking.
I would benefit from a clearer guide for what to escalate quickly versus what to batch.
The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.
A short weekly architecture review with someone senior would help me calibrate faster.
If I know exactly what ownership I should take this month, I can contribute with more confidence.
I will ask one architecture question in the next incident review instead of keeping it in my notes doc.
